message:
feat(ext): add rebuild_documents/rebuild_rows for FTS5 and R-tree rehydration

Virtual table extensions need a way to reconstruct their in-memory state
from persisted backing-table rows after a database is reopened or a
schema reload occurs. Without this, FTS5 inverted indexes and R-tree
spatial indexes are empty until the next insert.

FTS5 — Fts5Table::rebuild_documents(&mut self, rows):
  Clears the inverted index and document map, then re-inserts each
  (rowid, columns) pair via insert_document. Tracks next_rowid as
  max(rowid)+1 across all rows.

R-tree — RtreeVirtualTable::rebuild_rows(&mut self, rows):
  Replaces the entry vector with a freshly built index from persisted
  (rowid, values) pairs. Handles both formats: values with or without
  a leading id column (validates id matches rowid if present). Returns
  DatabaseCorrupt errors for mismatched id columns, wrong value counts,
  or duplicate entry ids.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
